title: Services, EndpointSlices, DNS, Discovery, and Traffic Policies · API Object Lens: K8s Pod Network Identity
summary: A frontend loses backends as Pods rotate because it pins Pod IPs; a Service selector overlaps a canary and traffic policies change source and locality behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s pod network identity increment in service discovery and endpoint evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain Pod IP identity, node routing, ports, host network exceptions, replacement, dual-stack boundaries, and application bind behavior.
Defend Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, CronJob, bare Pod, or custom controller selection from identity, completion, placement, update, and ownership evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Pod Network Identity · Theory K8s Pod Network Identity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Explain Pod IP identity, node routing, ports, host network exceptions, replacement, dual-stack boundaries, and application bind behavior.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Pod Network Identity contract for the supplied data.

For Predict K8s Pod Network Identity · Theory K8s Pod Network Identity, keep K8s Pod Network Identity connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Explain Pod IP identity, node routing, ports, host network exceptions, replacement, dual-stack boundaries, and application bind behavior. A defensible solution applies K8s Pod Network Identity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to service discovery and endpoint evidence map.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-services-endpoints-dns-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-services-endpoints-dns-3
    artifact: service-discovery.yaml
    decision: worked k8s services endpoints dns 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Service
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      type: ClusterIP
      selector: {app: status-api}
      endpoint-evidence: ready-serving-terminating
      dns: status-api.intake.svc.cluster.local
    verify: kubectl get service,endpointslice -l kubernetes.io/service-name=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject Pod IP pinning, short-name cross-namespace assumptions, Service-as-health-probe claims, or locality policy as automatic availability.
    repair: Align selectors, inspect EndpointSlice conditions, use qualified DNS, test bind and route layers, and document platform-specific exposure.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Containers in the same Pod need a Service to reach each other on localhost. This fails the K8s Pod Network Identity contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
